# Make Your Own Cache Driver

Warlock ships with several cache drivers out of the box:
- [Redis Cache Driver](./redis)
- [File Cache Driver](./file)
- [Memory Cache Driver](./memory)
- [Memory Extended Cache Driver](./memory-extended)
- [LRU Memory Cache Driver](./lru-memory)
- [Null Cache Driver](./null)

But you can easily make your own! All you need to do is implement the [CacheDriver interface](./cache-driver-interface), or extend [BaseCacheDriver](./base-cache-driver) for common logic.

## Example: Memcached Driver
Let's say we want to implement a [Memcached](https://memcached.org/) cache driver.

```ts
import { BaseCacheDriver, CacheDriver, parseCacheKey } from "@warlock.js/core";
import Memcached from "memcached";

export type MemCachedCacheDriverOptions = {
  host: string;
  port: number;
};

export class MemCachedCacheDriver
  extends BaseCacheDriver<Memcached, MemCachedCacheDriverOptions>
  implements CacheDriver<Memcached, MemCachedCacheDriverOptions>
{
  public name = "memcached";
  public async connect() {
    this.client = new Memcached(`${this.options.host}:${this.options.port}`);
    return this;
  }
  public async set(key, value) {
    const parsedKey = await this.parseKey(key);
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      this.client.set(parsedKey, value, 0, (error) => {
        if (error) return reject(error);
        resolve(true);
      });
    });
  }
  // ...implement other required methods
}
```

## Tips
- Extend [BaseCacheDriver](./base-cache-driver) to get logging, key parsing, and option handling for free.
- Use [parseCacheKey](./utils#parse-cache-key) for consistent key formatting.

See [Cache Driver Interface](./cache-driver-interface) for all required methods.
